response.justification: The candidate has genuine geospatial experience (founding GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang at Sociomantic, but the role's core requirement — modern C++ or Rust in a commercial setting — is only weakly met by a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments a decade ago. There is no navigation guidance or routing pipeline experience, and the recent career trajectory (TypeScript/Node full-stack, pricing platforms, AI tooling) points away from systems-level data pipeline engineering rather than toward it.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of GISCollective and Green Map contributor
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and demonstrated willingness to learn across the stack
concerns: Modern C++/Rust requirement essentially unmet — only a short C/C++ role in 2014–2015; DLang is adjacent but not equivalent
No experience with navigation systems, routing algorithms, or map-data tiling pipelines specifically
Recent focus (TypeScript/Node web and AI tooling) is far from systems-level geospatial pipeline work, suggesting a significant ramp-up
